_meta.note: KSA-first curated compliance matrix, v1. This is the product's moat per the idea doc, and per the plan's standing rules it needs an owner and a review date - NOT a substitute for legal/compliance sign-off. Regulatory requirements and provider certifications/regions change; verify current status before a report is shown to a customer for a regulated industry.
_meta.review_owner: unassigned - set before first paid engagement

PDPL (Saudi).requirement: Personal data of Saudi residents must be processed under PDPL principles; cross-border transfer requires an adequacy decision or SDAIA approval unless data stays in-Kingdom.
PDPL (Saudi).notes: Global hyperscalers may satisfy this only via a specific in-Kingdom region configured for data residency; confirm current regional availability and configuration before relying on this.

NCA ECC (Saudi).requirement: Applies to organizations operating Critical National Infrastructure; mandates a defined cybersecurity control baseline regardless of hosting location.
NCA ECC (Saudi).notes: ECC is a controls framework the customer must implement themselves; no cloud provider is 'ECC-compliant' on the customer's behalf. Flag as a shared-responsibility item, not a provider selector.

SAMA (Saudi).requirement: Saudi Central Bank cybersecurity and IT outsourcing framework for regulated financial entities; typically expects in-Kingdom hosting or an explicit SAMA no-objection for offshore cloud use.
SAMA (Saudi).notes: Financial-sector customers should treat this as a hard blocker pending explicit legal/regulator sign-off, not just a scoring adjustment.

CST cloud regulations (Saudi).requirement: Communications, Space & Technology Commission's Cloud Computing Regulatory Framework governs CSP registration tiers and customer data classification handling in KSA.
CST cloud regulations (Saudi).notes: Verify the specific provider's current CST registration tier for the customer's data classification; this changes over time.

GDPR.requirement: EU personal data requires an adequate legal basis for transfer outside the EEA (standard contractual clauses, an adequacy decision, or in-region processing).
GDPR.notes: All three operate EU regions and offer standard contractual clauses. Regional KSA-only providers are not typically applicable unless they have an EU presence.

HIPAA.requirement: US health data requires a signed Business Associate Agreement (BAA) with the hosting provider plus appropriate technical safeguards.
HIPAA.notes: Requires actively signing a BAA with the specific provider; not automatic just by choosing them.

PCI-DSS.requirement: Payment card data requires hosting on PCI DSS-attested infrastructure and a compliant application architecture.
PCI-DSS.notes: Provider infrastructure attestation is necessary but not sufficient; the customer's application and processes must also be assessed.

SOC 2.requirement: Service organization controls reporting; relevant when the customer's own product is audited and depends on subprocessor attestations.
SOC 2.notes: Major hyperscalers publish SOC 2 reports on request; confirm current report coverage dates.

ISO 27001.requirement: Information security management system certification, often required by enterprise customers as a vendor-selection gate.
ISO 27001.notes: Most major providers hold this at the infrastructure layer; the customer's own environment is certified separately.
